title: network ls: show networks in deterministic order
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

The new network backend stores the networks in a map so the returned
order is not deterministic. Lets sort the network names alphabetically
to ensure a deterministic order.
#### How to verify it

<!---
Please specify the precise conditions and/or the specific test(s) which must pass.
-->

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

<!--
Please uncomment this block and include only one of the following on a
line by itself:

None

-OR-

Fixes #<issue number>

*** Please also put 'Fixes #' in the commit and PR description***

-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM. How embarrassing that there are so few tests for `network ls` -- sorry about that!

Cause: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e993b88c03e1ef822532aacdbc7e6e381143afd3/contrib/cirrus/99-do-not-use-google-subnets.conflist#L3

This will need some reworking because system tests need to pass on developer environments, Fedora/Centos/RHEL gating, none of which have that magic `do-not-use` network.
@edsantiago What do you think about `is "$output" ".*$net1.*$net2.*$net3*podman.*"`? I think that should work even if there are other networks present.
That's very close to what I was thinking, and actually better: I was trying to include the newlines, but that really isn't necessary. Your approach is clean and reliable. LGTM.
